title: Integration tests carry a data-availability skip guard
evidence: internal/soccer/load_integration_test.go:16 t.Skipf("datasets not available") in loadRealKB; used by 7 integration tests (load_integration + query_integration). Datasets are present in this workspace (data/kaggle/*.csv) so the guard did NOT fire and the tests ran (defect_rate=1.0).
suggestion: Acceptable guard; note that in an environment without bundled data these 7 tests would silently skip rather than fail.

title: Statement coverage is moderate (58.5%)
evidence: test_coverage=0.585 from retort.db / scores.json. 50 test functions across 13 files; unit tests mirror each impl file one-to-one, integration tests assert against real data.
suggestion: Coverage is partial relative to the TDD prompt's 'thorough' goal; MCP server framing/error paths in server.go are less exercised than the soccer query engine.

title: TDD prompt outcome satisfied: comprehensive test-first structure
evidence: prompts/TDD.md requested test-first development with thorough coverage; result has 1,008 test LOC vs 1,594 impl LOC, one *_test.go per implementation file (parse/normalize/load/query/players/competition/dedup/stats), plus protocol and integration tests. The red-green-refactor *process* cannot be verified from the final artifact, but the resulting structure is consistent with it.
suggestion: No action — recorded for cross-run comparison of the prompt factor's effect.
